title: Robotic Color Sorting Conveyor
description: Arduino-controlled conveyor belt that sorts objects into three bins by colour using a TCS3200 sensor. A DC motor drives a short belt; a TCS3200 reads red, green, and blue when an object arrives at the sense point; the MCU classifies colour and actuates one of three servo diverter arms to route the object into the correct bin.
objective: Classify objects as RED, GREEN, or BLUE with > 90 % accuracy; sort rate ≥ 10 objects/min; conveyor belt 200 mm long; three output bins.
constraints: 5 V USB power; ≤ £25 BOM; Arduino Uno; 3D-printed belt frame and diverter arms; objects: 25 mm diameter coloured discs.
supporting_fact_titles: Color Sensing by Reflectance — TCS3200 Frequency-Mode Operation
DC Motor Speed Control via PWM
design_element_titles: TCS3200 Color Sensor for Product Sorting
DC Gear Motor with PWM Speed Control

element_usage_notes.TCS3200 Color Sensor for Product Sorting: TCS3200 mounted 12 mm above belt; white LED illumination (on S2/S3 select pulse); measures R/G/B frequency windows of 20 ms each; white-balance calibration at startup.
element_usage_notes.DC Gear Motor with PWM Speed Control: 6 V DC gear motor (100 RPM); L298N mini; PWM D6 = 40 % duty (~2.4 V, ~3 cm/s belt speed); direction fixed forward.
element_usage_notes.RC Servo Joint Actuator: Three SG90 micro-servos (diverter 1=D9, 2=D10, 3=D11); default 90° (neutral/pass); actuate to 0° or 180° for 300 ms to route object.
element_usage_notes.Arduino Uno/Nano MCU Platform: Uno R3; TCS3200 S0–S3 on D2–D5, OUT on D7 (timer interrupt); servo library for D9/D10/D11; L298N IN1/IN2/ENA on D6/D12/D13.

element_interactions.from: TCS3200 Color Sensor for Product Sorting
element_interactions.data: frequency counts for R, G, B channels (pulseIn)
element_interactions.description: Uno selects each filter (R: S2=0,S3=0; G: S2=1,S3=1; B: S2=0,S3=1), measures pulseIn duration for 20 ms window, normalises against white calibration, classifies as R/G/B by highest normalised channel.

element_interactions.to: DC Gear Motor with PWM Speed Control
element_interactions.data: PWM duty cycle (40 %) on D6 (ENA) + D12/D13 direction bits
element_interactions.description: Belt runs continuously at fixed slow speed. Motor halted for 200 ms during colour reading to prevent object drift on sensor.

element_interactions.data: Servo write (angle: 0°, 90°, or 180°) via Servo library
element_interactions.description: After classification: stop belt 50 ms; actuate corresponding diverter servo for 300 ms; return to 90°; resume belt. Diverter 1 = RED bin, 2 = GREEN bin, 3 = BLUE bin.
